mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over three months signals the onset of a recession, as firms begin widespread layoffs. This leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar mechanism to identify recessions in real-time.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained year-over-year decline in housing starts indicates a deep contraction in the residential investment sector, a key leading indicator of broader economic health. This weakness reduces aggregate demand and precipitates negative equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts peaked in early 2006 and have been in a steep decline for over a year, suggesting a severe housing bust.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 12-month change in HOUST and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is accelerating while the Federal Reserve is cutting the Fed Funds rate, it signals a policy conflict. This suggests the Fed sees significant economic weakness ahead that outweighs inflation concerns, implying a high probability of recession and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 3.5% AND 3-month change in FEDFUNDS < -0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed began cutting rates in September 2007 while inflation remained elevated.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change of industrial production is a direct measure of the health of the manufacturing and production sectors. A negative month-over-month change signals economic contraction, leading to lower earnings expectations and negative stock market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key coincident indicator used by the NBER to date business cycles.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, stronger-than-expected retail sales growth signals resilient consumer demand. This alleviates recession fears and boosts expectations for corporate revenues, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes the largest part of GDP.
failure_condition: When the Fed is easing, the correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth is a primary, high-frequency indicator of labor market health and overall economic momentum. Positive growth in PAYEMS signals robust economic activity, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive immediate equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly NFP release is a major market-moving event.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the 1-month change in PAYEMS for predicting 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.02 over a 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, it increases the cost of capital for businesses and the discount rate for future earnings. This compresses equity valuations and leads to negative returns over the medium term, even if the Fed has begun to ease slightly.
evidence_refs: fact:The current FEDFUNDS rate of 4.49% is historically restrictive, despite recent cuts from a peak of 5.25%.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% compared to when it is below that level (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and falling retail sales indicates a severe contraction in consumer health. Job losses reduce income while falling sales confirm a reduction in spending, creating a negative feedback loop for corporate revenues and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2 AND 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer weakness was a key driver of the 2001 recession.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances when both trigger conditions are met simultaneously.
